title: A review of the sirtuins family: pivotal regulators and emerging therapeutic targets in renal fibrosis.
snippet: Renal fibrosis represents the common pathological endpoint of chronic kidney disease (CKD) progression, yet effective therapies remain limited. title: FAS-controlled T cells drive lymphoproliferation through glycolysis without effector differentiation.
snippet: Lymphoproliferation in autoimmune lymphoproliferative syndrome (ALPS) due to FAS deficiency is driven by highly proliferative FAS-controlled T cells (FCT) with a distinct molecular signature. title: Cancer signaling networks in tumor progression and drug resistance: Crosstalk, adaptive reprogramming and therapeutic targeting (Review).
snippet: Cancer signaling networks, highly dynamic with interconnected systems, regulate tumor initiation, its progression, therapeutic response and drug resistance. title: Multi-omics profiling of pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors: Interplay of genomic instability and the tumor microenvironment (Review).

title: Interim CDER head Høeg is out days after Makary: report
snippet: Just a few days after FDA Commissioner Makary resigned, ally Tracy Beth Høeg is also leaving the agency. Her departure comes amid reports of tension over a commissioner's voucher for Sanofi's diabetes drug.

title: Takeda cuts 4,500, Novartis lays off biomedical research staff
snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

title: Employment and funding growth fell in 2025: California Life Sciences report
snippet: California life sciences jobs declined 1.8% last year, according to the new California Life Sciences sector report. While National Institutes of Health funding and venture capital investment rose, their growth slowed from the previous year.

title: Genmab drops 2 antibody assets, including another ProfoundBio ADC
snippet: Rina-S is the last candidate standing from Genmab’s $1.8 billion ProfoundBio acquisition two years ago, with the Danish drugmaker ending development of another clinical program stemming from the buyout.

title: Sun Pharma recalls cancer drug after finding glass particles in some vials
snippet: A batch of a chemotherapy product made at a Sun facility with a history of quality and compliance issues is being withdrawn from the U.S. market.

title: Biogen’s Alzheimer’s results bolster tau theory—and Denali’s next gen candidate
snippet: If Biogen has shown that tau can impact cognition, Denali’s technology—validated with an FDA approval in Hunter syndrome—could ensure the medicine gets where it needs to be for the greatest therapeutic impact, analysts said.

title: Future of Aardvark’s Prader-Willi drug in doubt as FDA slaps full hold on program
snippet: Aardvark Therapeutics had previously voluntarily suspended studies of ARD-101—and a related asset called ARD-201—after detecting anomalous echocardiographic readings in healthy volunteers that could indicate reduced heart efficiency.

title: CREATE jumps into CAR T competition with $122M series B
snippet: CREATE Medicines is working on a clinical-stage pipeline for cancer, while its autoimmune programs are still in preclinical testing.

title: GPCRs, radiopharma and the rise of functional peptide screening

title: REGENXBIO slumps after serious side effects mar Duchenne gene therapy results
snippet: Shares of REGENXBIO declined 37% on a mixed data readout and other updates from the company's first quarter earnings call Thursday.

title: Biogen takes tau Alzheimer’s therapy to Phase 3 despite mid-stage fail, prompting cautious optimism
snippet: While Biogen’s tau-targeting therapy didn’t demonstrate improvement on a dementia severity scale, the company touted biomarker and cognitive improvements from the Phase 2 study, leaving analysts eager for more data.

title: Biotech leaders pitch Pazdur as next FDA chief
snippet: In a letter to President Donald Trump, a group of biotech executives recommended former cancer regulator Richard Pazdur to lead the agency after the departure of Marty Makary.

title: Degron adds $40M to series A, supporting immunology plans for molecular glue platform
snippet: Degron Therapeutics will have stiff competition in immunology, as Novartis inked a $5.7 billion agreement with Monte Rosa Therapeutics last year to develop molecular glues for undisclosed immune-mediated conditions.

title: BeOne wins mantle cell lymphoma approval, opening new therapy class
snippet: Beqalzi is the first BCL2 inhibitor approved for relapsed or refractory mantle cell lymphoma.

title: Takeda’s ‘transformation program’ leaves 4,500 jobless
snippet: While Takeda is eliminating 4,500 roles across its global operations, the company has some 2,200 jobs currently open for hiring, for which internal candidates will be prioritized.
